factors.headline: UK-EU digital sovereignty regime tightens
factors.detail: Post-Brexit UK data adequacy decision under review by EU Commission; potential 2027 recertification risk. UK government Cloud-First mandate broadened to include AI workloads.

factors.headline: Vendor pricing inflation 7–12% YoY across the stack
factors.detail: Hyperscaler list prices up 9% (Microsoft), 12% (Oracle renewal terms); GenAI add-ons (Copilot, Now Assist, Agentforce) adding £200–400/user/year on top.
sources: Gartner IT Key Metrics Data, Q1 2026 (mock)

factors.headline: Hybrid-work tooling expectations re-set permanently
factors.detail: Employee NPS for collaboration tooling now a board-tracked KPI. Living-wage attestation increasingly demanded by works councils — gap for Atlassian, Oracle, Pega.

factors.headline: GenAI agents shift from pilot to procurement-grade
factors.detail: Agentforce, Copilot, Now Assist and Cortex AI all GA. Procurement faces new contractual questions: per-conversation pricing, model lineage, output IP ownership, and data-leakage liability.
sources: Gartner Hype Cycle for AI, Q1 2026 (mock)

factors.headline: EU AI Act enforcement starts; UK AI Bill in second reading
factors.detail: EU AI Act Article 6/9 obligations now binding on high-risk AI suppliers serving EU customers. UK Online Safety regime extending to embedded GenAI in enterprise tooling.
sources: EU AI Act enforcement guidance, Feb 2026 (mock)
UK AI Bill Hansard reading, March 2026 (mock)

factors.headline: Scope 3 software supply-chain reporting becomes mandatory
factors.detail: Centrica's net-zero commitment requires all Tier-1 IT suppliers to publish Scope 3 emissions. Gaps remain: Oracle, Pega, Snowflake (no Scope 3), Atlassian (partial).
